response:
TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY ADDING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. THE RADIUS OF EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE INCREASES BY 10% FROM THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE FORMULA FOR THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS PI TIMES RADIUS SQUARED. THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R, THE SECOND HAS A RADIUS OF 1.1R, THE THIRD 1.21R, AND SO ON. THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF THE AREAS OF ALL 8 CIRCLES. THIS IS DONE BY MULTIPLYING PI BY THE SUM OF THE SQUARES OF THE RADIUSES. THE FINAL EXPRESSION IS PI MULTIPLIED BY THE SUM OF R SQUARED, (1.1R) SQUARED, (1.21R) SQUARED, AND SO ON UNTIL THE EIGHTH CIRCLE. THIS GIVES THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IN TERMS OF R AND PI.

THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE FIFTH CIRCLE IS FOUND USING THE FORMULA 2 PI RADIUS. THE RADIUS OF THE FIFTH CIRCLE IS (1.4641R) AS EACH CIRCLE INCREASES BY 10%. THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS THEN 2 PI (1.4641R). TO PLACE TEXT ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE, EACH CHARACTER, INCLUDING SPACES, OCCUPIES A SPACE OF 0.5 CM. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS OBTAINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY 0.5. THIS GIVES A WHOLE NUMBER AS THE RESULT, ENSURING THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS AN INTEGER. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE FIFTH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY THIS CALCULATION. THE RESULT IS A WHOLE NUMBER, MAKING IT POSSIBLE TO EVENLY SPACE THE TEXT ALONG THE CIRCLE. THE FINAL ANSWER REPRESENTS THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE.
